page: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by two Intel Xeon 8124M processors. It features 72 virtual CPUs (36 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.67 GB of RAM per core. It contains no local storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieves a strong rating in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and a high PassMark CPU score. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 3741.32 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 212.97 GB/sec. Single-core performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations fall within the average tier. However, LLM text generation speeds are poor, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration for AI workloads.

A defining feature of the c5n.metal is its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, making it highly suitable for network-intensive applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on the 72 dedicated vCPUs for high-throughput workloads. This server is best suited for compute-heavy and network-bound workloads, including high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed database operations, and intensive network appliances.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for high-throughput networking and intensive CPU workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor, it delivers 72 vCPUs (36 physical cores) running at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, while single-core execution and database operations perform at average levels. Due to the absence of hardware accelerators, LLM text generation performance is poor. This bare-metal instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring raw compute power without virtualization overhead, making it an appropriate fit for high-performance computing, network appliances, batch processing, and distributed database nodes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance for network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 72 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring high-speed memory throughput and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding multi-core workloads.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8124M processors at 3.4 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead for maximum resource density
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, and batch processing
